{"step_by_step_thinking": "Enamel lamellae refer to the arrangement of enamel rods within the enamel. The documents discuss the organization of enamel in terms of prisms or rods, which are the basic components of enamel. There is no mention of enamel lamellae being elevations on the outer surface, dentinal tubules in enamel, or enamel projections in dentin. Therefore, the correct answer is the uniform arrangement of enamel rods.", "answer_choice": "C"}
